subject: Biology
title: Cancer Immunotherapy
description: A quick, practical guide to Cancer Immunotherapy.

hook.text: What if your own immune system could be reprogrammed to hunt and kill cancer cells?

buildup.text: Cancer cells evade immunity by displaying 'don't eat me' signals that fool T cells.

discovery.text: Checkpoint inhibitors are drugs that unmask cancer cells, letting T cells see and attack them.

twist.text: CAR-T therapy engineers a patient's own T cells with receptors that target their specific cancer.

climax.text: Some terminal cancer patients have gone into complete remission after immunotherapy—living years longer.

punchline.text: The cure might already be inside you. It just needs a nudge.

quiz.question: What do checkpoint inhibitor drugs do?
options: Unmask cancer cells so T cells can attack them
Strengthen the skin barrier against cancer
